title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite constellation for tracking airborne threats such as aircraft and cruise missiles. The network aims to enhance military surveillance and defense capabilities.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: Space Force's commercial gatekeeper emphasizes transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition for startups seeking defense contracts. The guidance highlights that these factors are as important as technical innovation.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace will collaborate on a 2028 demonstration mission to test air-breathing propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) geospatial imaging. The mission aims to validate advanced imaging capabilities in VLEO using innovative propulsion technology.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites has become a critical weapon in the US-Iran conflict, marking a shift toward geospatial warfare. The distinction between civilian and military use of such data is increasingly blurred.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch a new batch of rockets, including reusable models, with both state-led and commercial efforts aiming for debuts and first-stage recovery attempts in the near future.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test at Cape Canaveral on May 28, destroying the rocket and damaging the launch pad. The incident marks a significant setback for Blue Origin's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime, even if installations are attacked. These facilities aim to enhance the resilience and survivability of critical space operations.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Setting up a permanent lunar presence needs investment in biology
summary: Establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon requires significant investment in biological research and life support systems. The Apollo 17 mission in 1972 marked the last time humans walked on the lunar surface.

title: Revolv Space enters in-orbit servicing market with Infinite Orbits deal
summary: Revolv Space has entered the in-orbit servicing market by securing a deal with Infinite Orbits. The agreement involves Revolv Space providing Solar Array Drive Assemblies (SADAs) for Infinite Orbits' satellite operations.

title: 'Star City': Release date & how to watch Apple TV's superb Soviet space series
summary: 'Star City' is a new Apple TV spinoff series set in the Soviet space program, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the Cold War-era space race. The series is a spin-off of the popular show 'For All Mankind' and explores the history of Soviet cosmonautics.

title: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts return to Earth after being briefly 'stranded', wrapping up record-breaking mission (video)
summary: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts safely returned to Earth after a record-breaking mission, which included a brief 'stranding' incident. The mission marked a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31: Will you be able to see it?
summary: A rare Blue Moon will align with the red star Antares on May 31, creating a unique celestial event. Stargazers may witness this rare conjunction in the night sky.

title: 'For All Mankind' spin-off 'Star City' will make you want to know more about the Soviet space program
summary: The upcoming Apple TV+ spin-off 'Star City' explores the Soviet space program, offering a counterpart to NASA's Apollo narratives. The series aims to shed light on lesser-known stories from the Soviet era.

title: Atlas V rocket launches 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit from Florida
summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket launched 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites into orbit from Florida on May 28. This mission supports Amazon's growing satellite internet constellation.

title: Incredible videos show Blue Origin rocket explosion could be seen from hundreds of miles away
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during testing on May 28, creating a visible display of flames and debris across parts of Florida. The incident was witnessed from hundreds of miles away.

title: SpaceX launches 29 Starlink satellites from Florida just hours after Blue Origin rocket explosion (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 29 Starlink satellites from Florida, just hours after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during testing at the same spaceport. The close timing of the events highlighted contrasting fortunes for the two companies.

title: Record-breaking Europe heat wave from space | Space photo of the day for May 29, 2026
summary: A record-breaking heat wave in Europe is being observed from space, with satellite imagery showing extreme temperatures. The phenomenon is highlighted as the 'Space photo of the day' for May 29, 2026.

title: 'The Man Who Fell To Earth' at 50: Was David Bowie’s strange stranded alien his best role ever?
summary: The article reflects on David Bowie's iconic role as an alien in the 1976 film 'The Man Who Fell to Earth,' directed by Nicolas Roeg. It questions whether this performance remains his best role decades later.

title: Red dwarf stars are cosmic killers that eat their own planets
summary: Astronomers have found the first evidence that red dwarf stars can consume their own planets. This discovery highlights the destructive potential of these common but volatile stars.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to seven companies for construction and infrastructure improvements at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with a budget of up to $300 million. The upgrades will support mission facilities, utilities, and equipment, with all funds to be obligated by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA will host a public event featuring three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters in Washington, D.C. The event is part of the crew's postflight visit and will be held in the Webb Auditorium.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the primary mirror for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The 7.9-foot mirror will help capture detailed panoramas of cosmic objects.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness and faint appearance are highlighted against brighter foreground stars.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), which is located approximately 63 million light-years away. The galaxy is part of the Virgo Cluster and is moving toward its center.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa reveals crop types and their changes during the Southern Hemisphere's growing season, visualized with a vivid color palette.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is gearing up for its first supersonic flight as part of critical test flights. The mission aims to advance supersonic travel technology while addressing noise reduction.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA's Artemis human landing systems may face visibility issues during lunar landings due to dust clouds, as explained by aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs. This problem is compared to driving through dust on Earth.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, as confirmed by downlink data. The rover's recent drilling attempt follows a previous operation at 'Atacama'.

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: Scientists analyzed 20 Martian samples from NASA’s Curiosity Rover and found that hematite crystallite size variations could indicate ancient climate changes on Mars.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a significant anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a major setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for follow-up missions.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo mission among the highlights. The launches are expected to significantly expand satellite internet constellations.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been busy with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station. The mission marks another step in maintaining permanent occupancy of the station since June 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities. The mission impressed observers with its performance during its inaugural flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key development in SpaceX's future launch capabilities.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry topic. No specific details about the content or focus of the report were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring the durability of materials in the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is supported by these innovative minds.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests a focus on parachute testing or technology for space missions. The image title implies a thermal or baking test process for parachutes used in space exploration.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter diameter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life beneath the Martian surface while adhering to strict planetary protection measures.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance the safety and efficiency of space debris removal and satellite servicing missions.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during a launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the launch pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitions in the heavy-lift launch market.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe showcased progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event highlighted advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has secured $90 million in Series A funding to expand its laser and optical hardware production and international presence beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode after receiving an investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel propulsion technology. This funding aims to advance a new kind of thrust mechanism for the space industry.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform, significantly enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, signaling a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced the awards during a press conference, emphasizing the upcoming return to the lunar surface.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that in-space manufacturing in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic shift to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a nearly $4.2 billion contract for the initial phase of the SB-AMTI program. This follows a $2.3 billion contract for the Space Data Network Backbone awarded two days prior.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telecommunications giant Telenor Group.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards for the full year 2026, but missed stabilization targets in maritime and fixed broadband. The company also highlighted progress on its Equatys initiative.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's spaceflight timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for non-Earth imagery services, focusing on capturing images of objects in orbit. This contract highlights the growing demand for space-based surveillance and monitoring capabilities.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: UK’s O2 Satellite has expanded its direct-to-device (D2D) service to support iPhones. The service leverages SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation, which was launched earlier this year.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new appointments to drive global growth, including a chief commercial officer and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The topic gained attention following SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is questioning when it will achieve more efficient and reliable access to space, as discussed in a recent launch panel. The focus is on whether the European launch market will finally gain momentum.
